title: Quicksort
code:
quicksort :: Ord a => [a] -> [a]
quicksort [] = []
quicksort (x:xs) =
  quicksort [y | y <- xs, y <= x]
  ++ [x] ++ quicksort [y | y <- xs, y > x]
description: The canonical Haskell quicksort — often the first example shown to newcomers. Uses list comprehensions to partition around a pivot. Elegant but not in-place; allocates new lists at each level.
